item.title: Legge expects physical contest as Drua Women host Reds tomorrow at 1pm in Lautoka
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women head coach Mike Legge is expecting a tough and physical contest when his side takes on the Queensland Reds in tomorrow's Super Rugby Women's semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka. The Drua secured the right to host the semi-final after finishing at the top of the regular season standings and will face a Reds side Legge believes will present a far greater challenge than
item.content:encoded:
McDonald's Fiji Drua Women head coach Mike Legge is expecting a tough and physical contest when his side takes on the Queensland Reds in tomorrow's Super Rugby Women's semi-final at Churchill Park in Lautoka.

The Drua secured the right to host the semi-final after finishing at the top of the regular season standings and will face a Reds side Legge believes will present a far greater challenge than in their previous meetings.

Speaking ahead of the match, Legge says semi-final rugby is a different proposition and expects the Reds to arrive well prepared.

He says the Reds will not be the same team the Drua faced during the pre-season or in the final round-robin match.

Legge also praised Reds head coach Andrew Fraser, describing him as a smart and experienced coach who would have closely analysed the Drua's performances throughout the season.

He says his coaching staff have also studied the Reds, making for what he expects to be a highly competitive encounter.

He adds that the match will be a fitting showcase for semi-final rugby.

The McDonald's Fiji Drua Women will host the Queensland Reds at 1pm tomorrow at Churchill Park in Lautoka.

The second semi-final will see the Western Force take on the NSW Waratahs at 6.05pm.

The winner will progress to next week's Super Rugby Women's final.

item.title: Vakatawa to make Flying Fijians debut against Scotland
Labasa looking to extend EPL lead with home clash against Suva
item.description: Extra Supermarket Labasa FC will be aiming to tighten their grip on the Extra Premier League summit when they host Suva FC at Subrail Park tomorrow. The Babasiga Lions have turned Subrail Park into a fortress this season, with their passionate fans playing a key role in the team's success.
item.content:encoded:
Extra Supermarket Labasa FC will be aiming to tighten their grip on the Extra Premier League summit when they host Suva FC at Subrail Park tomorrow.

The Babasiga Lions have turned Subrail Park into a fortress this season, with their passionate fans playing a key role in the team's success.

A victory over Suva would further strengthen the northern side's position at the top of the table.

The remaining fixtures will be played on Sunday.

Tailevu Naitasiri FC will face Nadi FC at 12pm at HFC Bank Stadium, before Rewa FC take on Nadroga FC at 2pm at the same venue.

Lautoka FC host Nasinu FC at 1pm at Churchill Park, while Ba FC take on Navua FC at 3pm at 4R Stadium, Govind Park.

In the current standings, Labasa lead with 28 points, followed by Ba on 27. Rewa are third with 25 points, while Lautoka are fourth with 20.

item.title: MGM High School claims U15 title after edging DAV College 6-5 on penalties
item.description: MGM High School have been crowned the Suva Secondary Schools Soccer Association Under-15 champions after defeating DAV College 6-5 in a penalty shootout in the final. The final ended 0-0 after normal time, with both teams unable to break the deadlock.
item.content:encoded:
MGM High School have been crowned the Suva Secondary Schools Soccer Association Under-15 champions after defeating DAV College 6-5 in a penalty shootout in the final.

The final ended 0-0 after normal time, with both teams unable to break the deadlock.

Extra time also failed to produce a goal, sending the match to penalties.

MGM held their nerve in the shootout to claim a 6-5 victory and the Under-15 title.

The win also secures MGM High School a place in the Southern Zone playoffs.

Source: Fiji Secondary School Football Association

item.title: England players disappointed with Tuchel's tactics after semi-final loss
item.description: Some England players were reportedly disappointed with manager Thomas Tuchel's tactics following their 2-1 FIFA World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina. BBC Sport reports that several key players felt the team's defensive approach after taking a 1-0 lead with 35 minutes remaining allowed Argentina to take control of the match.
item.content:encoded:
Some England players were reportedly disappointed with manager Thomas Tuchel's tactics following their 2-1 FIFA World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina.

BBC Sport reports that several key players felt the team's defensive approach after taking a 1-0 lead with 35 minutes remaining allowed Argentina to take control of the match.

The report says Tuchel switched to a back five to protect the lead, but Argentina scored twice late in the game to book a place in the final.

Former England captain Wayne Rooney also criticised Tuchel, saying he believed the defeat was the result of the manager's decisions.

Meanwhile, England will face France in the FIFA World Cup third-place play-off at 9am on Sunday.

The FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain will kick off at 7am on Monday.

item.title: No matter how successful I am on the field, family keeps me grounded - Messi
item.description: The 39-year-old, who captained Argentina to the 2022 FIFA World Cup title, says he tries to live a normal life with his wife, Antonela Roccuzzo, and their three sons, Thiago, Matteo and Ciro. Messi says he spends time with his family, disciplines his children and shares the same responsibilities as any other parent.
item.content:encoded:
The 39-year-old, who captained Argentina to the 2022 FIFA World Cup title, says he tries to live a normal life with his wife, Antonela Roccuzzo, and their three sons, Thiago, Matteo and Ciro.

Messi says he spends time with his family, disciplines his children and shares the same responsibilities as any other parent.

He says despite the fame and recognition he receives around the world, his family helps keep him grounded.

Messi and Roccuzzo first met as children in their hometown of Rosario, Argentina, and have been together since their teenage years. They married in 2017 and have three sons together.

item.title: Natabua High School student ready to represent Fiji at first Commonwealth Games
item.description: The Year 13 student of Natabua High School says her preparations have been going well, although she is still working on improving a few areas of her performance before the competition. Lutumailagi, who trains with the Nadi Athletics Club, says one of her biggest challenges has been travelling between Nadi and Suva for training sessions with the rest of the national squad.
item.content:encoded:
The Year 13 student of Natabua High School says her preparations have been going well, although she is still working on improving a few areas of her performance before the competition.

Lutumailagi, who trains with the Nadi Athletics Club, says one of her biggest challenges has been travelling between Nadi and Suva for training sessions with the rest of the national squad.

She says despite the long journeys, she has remained focused on her goal of representing Fiji on one of the biggest sporting stages.

Lutumailagi says fellow athlete Makelesi Bulikiobo and her father have been her biggest inspirations throughout her athletics journey.

The Fiji athlete will compete in the women's 800 metres at the 2026 Commonwealth Games in Glasgow, Scotland.

The 23rd Commonwealth Games will be held in Glasgow from next Friday to 2nd August.

item.title: Seruvakula urges patience as Flying Fijians continue to build
item.description: The FIJI Water Flying Fijians are still in the building phase, but the bond within the squad remains strong. This was shared to fijivillage Sports by Flying Fijians Head Coach, Senirusi Seruvakula, from England, saying the players are well connected and have developed a tight relationship within the camp.
item.content:encoded:
The FIJI Water Flying Fijians are still in the building phase, but the bond within the squad remains strong.

This was shared to fijivillage Sports by Flying Fijians Head Coach, Senirusi Seruvakula, from England, saying the players are well connected and have developed a tight relationship within the camp.

He urged the people of Fiji to have faith in his leadership and the team, stressing that success will take time.

Seruvakula says fans need to be patient, adding that the results will come as the team continues to grow and improve.

He also says the squad is focused on lifting its performance ahead of its match against Scotland.

The Flying Fijians will play Scotaland this Sunday at 1.10am.

In other matches, the All Blacks will host Ireland at 7.10am at Eden Park on Saturday, Japan will meet France at 8.40pm, and the Wallabies will face the Italy at 10.10pm.

On Sunday, South Africa will meet Wales at 3.40am, and England will meet Argentina at 7.10am.

item.title: Naisewa to line-up along sister for 30th Drua appearance this Saturday against the Reds
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women prop Karalaini Naisewa will reach a major milestone this weekend when the side hosts the Queensland Red

item.description: The Junior Bula Boys have taken a big step towards the semi-finals of the OFC U-16 Men's Championship after thrashing Samoa 5-0 in

item.title: Let's make it uncomfortable for the visiting team with a sellout crowd on Saturday - Legge
item.description: McDonald's Fiji Drua Women coach Mike Legge is calling on fans from around Fiji to pack Churchill Park this Saturday as his side l

item.title: Pressure is on Argentina - England defender Marc Guehi
item.description: England defender Marc Guehi insists the pressure is on Argentina as the Three Lions prepare to face the reigning champions in the
